If the project has no current parent or lacks an associated organization resource, resourcemanager.projects.setIamPolicy permission is also required.

message: Operation 'cloudresourcemanager.folders.create' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Missing summary. Description is extremely verbose with formatting issues ('+' symbols). Reorganized to clearly separate: action, return type, constraints, error handling, and permissions. Better structured for AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Creates a folder in the resource hierarchy. Returns an `Operation` which can be used to track the progress of the folder creation workflow. Upon success, the `Operation.response` field will be populated with the created Folder. In order to succeed, the addition of this new folder must not violate the folder naming, height, or fanout constraints. + The folder's `display_name` must be distinct from all other folders that share its parent. + The addition of the folder must not cause the active folder hierarchy to exceed a height of 10. Note, the full active + deleted folder hierarchy is allowed to reach a height of 20; this provides additional headroom when moving folders that contain deleted folders. + The addition of the folder must not cause the total number of folders under its parent to exceed 300. If the operation fails due to a folder constraint violation, some errors may be returned by the `CreateFolder` request, with status code `FAILED_PRECONDITION` and an error description. Other folder constraint violations will be communicated in the `Operation`, with the specific `PreconditionFailure` returned in the details list in the `Operation.error` field. The caller must have `resourcemanager.folders.create` permission on the identified parent.
data.summary_suggestion: Create a new folder in resource hierarchy
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new folder as a child of the specified parent resource in the resource hierarchy. Returns a long-running Operation that can be tracked to monitor folder creation progress. Upon success, Operation.response contains the created Folder object. The operation enforces constraints: folder display_name must be unique among siblings, hierarchy height cannot exceed 10 levels, and parent cannot have more than 300 child folders. Violations return FAILED_PRECONDITION errors with details in Operation.error. Requires `resourcemanager.folders.create` permission on the parent resource.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is extremely verbose with formatting issues ('+' symbols). Reorganized to clearly separate: action, return type, constraints, error handling, and permissions. Better structured for AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

Added information about how filters are provided and clarified eventual consistency behavior for AI agents to understand potential delays in seeing newly created organizations.

data.current_description: Searches organization resources that are visible to the user and satisfy the specified filter. This method returns organizations in an unspecified order. New organizations do not necessarily appear at the end of the results, and may take a small amount of time to appear. Search will only return organizations on which the user has the permission `resourcemanager.organizations.get` or has super admin privileges.
data.summary_suggestion: Search organizations by filter criteria
data.description_suggestion: Searches for organizations matching the specified filter criteria that are visible to and accessible by the caller. Returns organizations in an unspecified order with eventual consistency; newly created organizations may take time to appear in results. Only returns organizations where the caller has `resourcemanager.organizations.get` permission or super admin privileges. Filter criteria are specified via query parameters.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks details about filter specification and eventual consistency implications. Added information about how filters are provided and clarified eventual consistency behavior for AI agents to understand potential delays in seeing newly created organizations.
